name: Chocolate Chiffon Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the pastry shell by processing flour and salt in a food processor.
2. Add Crisco and mix until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
3. Gradually add iced water through the feed tube until the dough holds together but is not wet or sticky (should not take more than 30 seconds).
4. Turn out the dough onto a sheet of plastic wrap, top with another piece, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
5. Roll the dough between two pieces of waxed paper to fit a 10-inch pie plate. Remove the top layer of waxed paper and place the pastry into the pie plate.
6. Remove the paper, flute the edge, and prick the bottom with a fork. Bake at 450°F for 10-12 minutes until set.
7. Prepare the filling by softening gelatin in cold water for 5 minutes.
8. Melt chocolate chips and coffee in a double boiler until smooth, stirring constantly.
9. Remove from heat, add the softened gelatin, and stir until dissolved.
10. Beat egg yolks well. Add yolks, 1/4 cup of sugar, salt, and vanilla to the melted mixture.
11. In a separate bowl, beat egg whites until foamy. Gradually add the remaining 1/4 cup of sugar and beat until stiff peaks form.
12. Gently fold the beaten egg whites into the chocolate mixture and pour into the prepared pastry shell.
13. Chill the pie for several hours or overnight until firm.
14. Top with whipped cream before serving.
This recipe freezes well and tastes great.
